query:
INSERT INTO relays (number, name, subtitle, emoji, era, quote, quoteAuthor, narrative, missionObjective, sagePath, builderPath, webType, energy, tier, xpReward, inventionCount) VALUES
(1, 'Fire', 'The Eternal Constant', '🔥', 'pre-10,000 BCE', 'The most powerful weapon on earth is the human soul on fire.', 'Ferdinand Foch', 'Twelve thousand years ago, a great change erupted of unimaginable proportions. In an explosion of intent, man rose from hunter and prey to apex predator. The seed was sown, the threads spun, and the age of intelligence began. Civilisation ignited — an epic of incalculable possibility.', 'Understand the fundamental role of controlled fire in early human survival and societal development.', 'Analyze the shift in human behavior and social structures enabled by the mastery of fire. Discuss its dual nature as a force of creation and destruction.', 'Identify the key innovations associated with fire (e.g., hearth, early metallurgy) and explain how each contributed to human progress.', 'Natural', 'Primal Radiant', 'student', 443000, 9),
(2, 'Tree', 'The Living Foundation', '🌳', '10,000–5,000 BCE', 'The creation of a thousand forests is in one acorn.', 'Ralph Waldo Emerson', 'From the first felled timber to the last great mast, wood was the skeleton of civilisation. Shelter, tools, ships, bridges — the tree gave humanity its first scalable building material and the confidence to reshape the landscape.', 'Explore how timber and woodcraft became the first scalable construction technology.', 'Examine how the transition from nomadic to settled life depended on wood as a structural material.', 'Identify key wooden structures and innovations (longhouses, palisades, early bridges) and their role in settlement.', 'Natural', 'Rooted Growth', 'student', 520000, 8),
(3, 'River', 'Cradles of Continuity', '🌊', '5,000–3,000 BCE', 'Civilisation has been a permanent dialogue between human beings and water.', 'Paolo Lugari', 'The great rivers — Nile, Tigris, Euphrates, Indus, Yellow — became the arteries of early civilisation. Irrigation, transport, trade, and governance all flowed from the mastery of water.', 'Understand how river systems enabled the first complex civilisations.', 'Analyze how hydraulic engineering shaped governance, agriculture, and urban planning in river valley civilisations.', 'Map the key innovations in water management (irrigation canals, aqueducts, dams) across early civilisations.', 'Natural', 'Flowing Current', 'student', 610000, 8),
(4, 'Horse', 'The Velocity of Intent', '🐎', '3,000–1,000 BCE', 'A horse is worth more than riches.', 'Spanish Proverb', 'The domestication of the horse transformed warfare, trade, communication, and the very concept of distance. Mounted civilisations could project power, connect empires, and accelerate the diffusion of ideas across continents.', 'Examine how horse domestication revolutionised mobility, warfare, and trade networks.', 'Discuss the geopolitical implications of mounted warfare and the horse-powered logistics of ancient empires.', 'Identify key equestrian innovations (chariot, saddle, stirrup, postal relay) and their civilisational impact.', 'Machine', 'Kinetic Surge', 'student', 720000, 7),
(5, 'Roads', 'Arteries of Intent', '🛤️', '1,000 BCE–500 CE', 'All roads lead to Rome.', 'Ancient Proverb', 'Roads are the physical manifestation of political will. From the Persian Royal Road to the Roman network, engineered pathways enabled trade, military logistics, cultural exchange, and the administrative reach of empires.', 'Understand how engineered road networks became instruments of imperial power and economic integration.', 'Analyze how road infrastructure influenced the rise and fall of empires, comparing Roman, Persian, and Chinese approaches.', 'Identify key road engineering innovations (paving, drainage, bridges, milestones) and their lasting influence.', 'Machine', 'Directed Force', 'student', 850000, 8),
(6, 'Ships', 'The Master Weaver''s Reach', '⛵', '500–1500 CE', 'He that would learn to pray, let him go to sea.', 'George Herbert', 'Ships extended civilisation beyond the horizon. From Viking longships to Chinese treasure fleets, maritime technology enabled exploration, colonisation, global trade, and the first truly interconnected world economy.', 'Explore how maritime technology expanded civilisational reach across oceans.', 'Compare the maritime strategies of different civilisations (Viking, Arab, Chinese, European) and their global impact.', 'Identify key shipbuilding innovations (keel, lateen sail, compass, caravel) and their role in global exploration.', 'Machine', 'Oceanic Momentum', 'student', 980000, 9),
(7, 'Loom', 'The Binary Birth', '🧶', '1500–1760 CE', 'The loom is the mother of all machines.', 'Lewis Mumford', 'The loom was the first programmable machine. From Jacquard''s punch cards to the binary logic of modern computing, textile technology planted the seed of the information age within the industrial revolution.', 'Understand how textile machinery became the prototype for programmable technology.', 'Trace the conceptual lineage from the Jacquard loom to modern computing, examining the role of binary logic.', 'Identify key textile innovations (spinning jenny, power loom, Jacquard mechanism) and their industrial impact.', 'Digital', 'Woven Pattern', 'student', 1100000, 7),
(8, 'Rail', 'Standardizing the Continental Rhythm', '🚂', '1760–1870 CE', 'What can be more palpably absurd than the prospect held out of locomotives travelling twice as fast as stagecoaches?', 'The Quarterly Review, 1825', 'Railways standardised time, unified markets, and compressed geography. The iron horse was the first infrastructure to operate at industrial scale, demanding new forms of engineering, finance, and governance.', 'Examine how railways transformed economics, timekeeping, and national identity.', 'Analyze how railway infrastructure demanded new approaches to engineering, finance, and standardisation.', 'Identify key railway innovations (steam locomotive, standard gauge, signal systems, steel rail) and their systemic effects.', 'Machine', 'Rhythmic Pulse', 'student', 1300000, 8),
(9, 'Engine', 'The Internal Revolution', '⚙️', '1870–1914 CE', 'The automobile has not merely taken over the street, it has dissolved the living tissue of the city.', 'Lewis Mumford', 'The internal combustion engine democratised mobility. Cars, trucks, and tractors reshaped cities, agriculture, warfare, and the very rhythm of daily life — creating both unprecedented freedom and profound dependency.', 'Understand how the internal combustion engine reshaped society, cities, and global logistics.', 'Discuss how automobile infrastructure influenced urban planning, suburban sprawl, and energy dependency.', 'Identify key automotive innovations (assembly line, highway system, fuel infrastructure) and their societal transformation.', 'Machine', 'Combustive Drive', 'student', 1500000, 7),
(10, 'AAA Triad', 'The Triple Convergence', '✈️', '1914–1969 CE', 'The airplane stays up because it doesn''t have the time to fall.', 'Orville Wright', 'Aviation, Automobile, and Airwaves converged to create the modern connected world. Flight conquered distance, broadcasting unified culture, and the automobile defined personal freedom — together they rewired civilisation.', 'Explore how the convergence of aviation, automotive, and broadcast technologies created the modern world.', 'Analyze how the AAA Triad reshaped warfare, commerce, culture, and the concept of distance in the 20th century.', 'Identify key innovations across all three domains and explain their synergistic effects on civilisation.', 'Digital', 'Convergent Wave', 'student', 1700000, 9),
(11, 'Orbit', 'The Programmable Frontier', '🛰️', '1969–2025 CE', 'The Earth is the cradle of humanity, but mankind cannot stay in the cradle forever.', 'Konstantin Tsiolkovsky', 'Satellites, the internet, and programmable systems extended infrastructure into orbit and cyberspace. The digital revolution made every human a potential node in a planetary network of information and commerce.', 'Understand how orbital and digital infrastructure created the networked civilisation.', 'Discuss how satellite technology, the internet, and programmable systems transformed governance, commerce, and identity.', 'Identify key orbital and digital innovations (GPS, internet protocols, cloud computing) and their civilisational scale.', 'Digital', 'Orbital Frequency', 'student', 1900000, 8),
(12, 'Human Nodes', 'The Torus Metaphor', '🧠', '2025+ CE', 'We are the infrastructure.', 'Nigel T. Dearden', 'The final relay is humanity itself. As biological intelligence merges with artificial systems, each person becomes a node in a living infrastructure — the torus of consciousness where input, processing, and output flow in an infinite loop.', 'Contemplate the future where humans themselves become the infrastructure of civilisation.', 'Explore the philosophical and practical implications of human-AI integration and the concept of consciousness as infrastructure.', 'Envision the technologies and social structures needed for the Human Nodes era (neural interfaces, collective intelligence, ethical AI).', 'Consciousness', 'Toroidal Flow', 'student', 2400000, 12);
